questions.question: In a distributed system, a group of processes coordinate their actions to achieve a common goal. Explain the concept of 'distributes mutual exclusion' and provide an example of how this concept can be applied in group communication.
questions.hint: Distributes mutual exclusion is a concept used in distributed systems to ensure that only one process can access a shared resource at a time.

questions.question: A team of software developers is working on a project. They need to agree on the implementation details of a new feature. Describe the importance of coordination and agreement in group communication in this scenario.
questions.hint: In group communication, coordination and agreement are essential for ensuring that all team members are on the same page and that the project progresses smoothly.

questions.question: In a distributed system, a group of processes may need to make decisions collectively. Explain the concept of 'coordination protocols' and list two common types of coordination protocols used in group communication.
questions.hint: Coordination protocols are used to synchronize the actions of multiple processes in a distributed system. Two common types of coordination protocols are token rings and semaphore algorithms.
